_comment: Single source of truth for Pulp's minimum supported OS per platform.
A consumer's floor = the MAX minimum among the prebuilt libraries it links.
'measured' is read from the shipped artifact (otool -l minos on Apple; objdump -T GLIBC max on Linux;
dumpbin on Windows). 'expected' is Google's upstream value for the pinned Skia/Dawn milestone
(Chromium build/config/mac/mac_sdk.gni mac_deployment_target, etc.). A measured != expected is drift.
'always_linked' deps always count toward the floor; V8 counts only when PULP_JS_ENGINE=v8.
A dep with 'toolchain_declared':true (e.g. libcxx) has no shippable artifact to otool — its floor is a
known toolchain constant (Apple SDK availability annotation), so measure_min_os.py leaves it untouched.
Regenerate with tools/scripts/measure_min_os.py after any Skia/Dawn/V8 pin bump.
CMake reads this before project() via tools/cmake/PulpMinOs.cmake to pin the deployment target.

macos-arm64.note: Floor is set by Apple's libc++, not Skia/Dawn: std::format instantiates floating-point formatters through its type-erased visitor for EVERY format call (even integer-only ones), and the macOS 15.4 SDK marks std::to_chars(float/double/long double) unavailable before macOS 13.4. Pulp's logging (core/runtime/log.hpp) uses std::format pervasively, so 13.4 is the cross-SDK toolchain floor above Google's 13.0 deps target. Verified against the macOS 15.4 SDK by compiling the same std::format probe at deployment target 13.3 (fails) vs 13.4 (builds).

libcxx.source: Apple libc++ std::to_chars(float/double/long double) availability in the macOS 15.4 SDK (usr/include/c++/v1/__charconv/to_chars_floating_point.h), reached via std::format in core/runtime/log.hpp

macos-x64.note: Intel (x86_64) macOS target. Floor is arch-independent and set by Apple's libc++, not Skia/Dawn: std::to_chars(float/double/long double) — reached via std::format in core/runtime/log.hpp — is marked unavailable before macOS 13.4 in the macOS 15.4 SDK, exactly as on macos-arm64. The x86_64 Skia + Dawn prebuilt slices (danielraffel/skia-builder chrome/m151-minos13, skia-build-mac-x86_64-gpu-release.zip) both stamp minos 13.0 (measured via measure_min_os.py --measure over the unpacked libskia.a / libdawn_combined.a on 2026-07-10), below the 13.4 toolchain floor. A universal (arm64;x86_64) build's floor is max(macos-arm64, macos-x64) = 13.4.

skia.source: skia-builder chrome/m151-minos13 mac-x86_64 slice; measure_min_os.py --measure over build/mac-gpu/lib/Release/x86_64/libskia.a

dawn.source: skia-builder chrome/m151-minos13 mac-x86_64 slice; measure_min_os.py --measure over build/mac-gpu/lib/Release/x86_64/libdawn_combined.a (bundled in the Skia toolchain)

libcxx.source: Apple libc++ std::to_chars(float/double/long double) availability in the macOS 15.4 SDK (arch-independent), reached via std::format in core/runtime/log.hpp

v8.source: v8-builder build-v8.py mac_deployment_target (single mac target across arm64/x86_64 slices)

ios-arm64.note: Deps allow iOS 14 (Skia/Dawn stamp 14.0); Pulp declares 16.0 via IOS_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET. Toolchain-declared, not leaked. V8 excluded (JSC-only on iOS).

skia.source: skia-builder IOS_MIN_VERSION (Dawn needs iOS 14 for std::atomic wait)

linux-x64.note: Floor = glibc 2.34, measured off the shipped artifacts (v8: objdump -T libv8.so; skia/dawn: --whole-archive link-probe over libskia.a + libdawn_combined.a, since static .a undefined refs are unversioned). The prebuilts are built in an Ubuntu 22.04 container (portable-linux-x64 jobs in skia-builder build-skia.yml + v8-builder build-v8.yml) to keep the floor low; the DEFAULT ubuntu-latest lanes leak glibc ~2.39. 22.04 (gcc-11) is the oldest clean C++20 base: V8 must use the platform libstdc++ (its public API exposes std:: types; bundled libc++ breaks the drop-in ABI), and Skia is C++20 too. Loadable on RHEL/Rocky 9+, Ubuntu 22.04+, Debian 12+, Fedora 35+. IMPORTANT: a consumer's final binary also includes ITS OWN compiled objects — to actually ship a 2.34 floor, Pulp's own Linux build must run on the same <=2.34 base (ubuntu:22.04); the PulpMinOs.cmake post-link drift-check catches a build host that re-leaks a higher glibc.

skia.source: skia-builder build-skia.yml portable-linux-x64 job (Ubuntu 22.04 container); --whole-archive link-probe floor

dawn.source: skia-builder build-skia.yml portable-linux-x64 job (Ubuntu 22.04 container); bundled in libdawn_combined.a

v8.source: v8-builder build-v8.yml portable-linux-x64 job (Ubuntu 22.04 container); objdump -T libv8.so

windows-x64.note: Floor = Windows 10 (subsystem version 10.0; API level _WIN32_WINNT/WINVER = 0x0A00), Google's Chromium default at the pinned Skia/Dawn milestone (build/config/win/BUILD.gn, verified 2026-07-08 against Chromium source). Unlike macOS+libc++ there is no std::format availability bump: Pulp's Windows build uses MSVC's header-only STL. Pulp does not define _WIN32_WINNT itself, so it otherwise inherits the Windows SDK default (a newer SDK could silently raise the floor — the Windows analog of the macOS Dawn leak). PulpMinOs.cmake pins _WIN32_WINNT/WINVER to 'win32_winnt'. The comparable 'floor'/'expected'/'measured' fields use the OS/subsystem version (10.0); _WIN32_WINNT is a compile-time define not recorded in the shipped static .libs, so 'measured' stays null until a Windows host runs measure_min_os.py --write over a built Pulp PE (subsystem version via dumpbin /headers or llvm-readobj).

skia.source: Chromium _WIN32_WINNT/WINVER 0x0A00 (Windows 10) default (build/config/win/BUILD.gn); skia-builder win path sets no override
